Low.

Ok.

is?

Nice.
Nice.
It's awesome.
Yeah, Sandra joining as well.
Yes.
All right.
We'll let her get, let her get in.
Sarah remind me what is already?
Hi, Sandra.
Hey, Sandra,
what is your role at, um, the company?
Princeton, if you don't mind.
Sort of sharing a little bit like introductions a little bit about yourself and kind of, we'll go into what you're, we're looking to achieve and provide an introduction today.
Awesome, great.
I'm the Chief marketing Officer here.
I've been at Princeton for this is my 29th year here.
So my areas within the company would be anything that's touching revenue generation, sales, marketing, uh technology, software, um trainings, that kind of stuff.
So people and money and technology would be my camp.
Yeah, perfect, perfect.
Well, my name is Adam again, like I already introduced myself.
I'm you guys' rep over here at Department IQ and uh Justin on the call as well as our product specialist, which will be going into the platform today again.
Thank you for your time.
Um Sandra, what is your role at Princeton?
Yeah,
I'm um our digital marketing specialist.
So I um work with Sarah on pretty much anything digital marketing related.
So in terms of getting you guys api access, anything else regarding that is something that I would be taking care of?
Perfect.
Perfect.
Um I always kind of like to start off these calls.
I know we've been kind of going back and forth over a couple of months and um sounds like this is just a little bit better timing to where we finally got this on the calendar.
But um what sort of drove you to take the meeting with us today and I guess we kind of want to get a better understanding of what you're looking to achieve going forward from a dated perspective.
Sure.
Um full transparency.
What drove us to this meeting was your tenacity.
Yeah.

Uh we uh we haven't been looking for a new platform but um uh and unfortunately you're right, you are hitting me on, on times that were just, just not good times for us and for whatever reason you have my personal cell phone.
So when you call, it's always when I'm picking my son up from something or when it, so it's always a really weird time.
Like I can, we talk some other time is really strange.
Um But yes, no, we're looking forward to learning more about your offerings and to see if it's something that uh should be incorporated into our world.
OK, perfect.
What would you mind sharing a little bit about your current market survey process?
Um Because how you're going about collecting data and maybe some tools that you have internally that are helping to enable that process
uh for we partner with a few different companies that uh
trying to think of some that would be overlapping.

intrata is our tenant management software.
So we use them for a lot of our surveying and outreach.
We have satisfaction on retainer.
We use them for a lot of prospecting of information and surveying.
Um And then just other
analytics that we get from other Softwares like Google Analytics or things like that would be how we uh get our information for marketing decisions,
any sort of manual process as far as calling around to comps or manually going to property websites, anything like that,

not really the, uh the cras um that we do at the site level, that stuff is really up to the site teams to usually web based product that we can build those cras from every once in a while to validate um the integrity of the information we would get on the phone and call through, but generally it would be just web, web driven.
Ok.
Ok.
Um And, and what about revenue management?
Anything in place that you guys are utilizing to help you go about pricing your units?
Yeah.
No, we don't use any pricing tools.
We have dabbled with them in the past.
Um Just given our footprint, our regional area and how long a lot of my colleagues and I have been here.
Um We're just not a huge fan of it in our region.

Uh If, if we spread Adam to, you know, if all of a sudden I have a giant portfolio in Ohio and I can't really put a finger on the pulse of rental market there, then maybe we'd need some pricing tools, but we've dabbled with them in the past.
We've put them in place and a couple of our assets for A B testing, but um they're not for us at this point.
Awesome.
Awesome.
Um Well, are you guys all owner operated or solely third party management?
We are about 75% owner managed and about 25% fee based.
Ok,
perfect, perfect.
Um Justin, do you have any questions before we kind of dive
in?
Not really.
I was like, you guys have, what?
Close to 6000 units throughout the Boston area, is that about right?
Yeah, we're uh hitting about 7000 this month.
But yes, pretty much nice.
How's the, uh, the market up there as far as concessions go,
you know, Justin, it's, uh, it's, they've come back
for the, uh, you know, the past few years I think all of us were gleaning off that long tail after the pandemic.
And so it was a nice ride we had for three or four years.
Um, nothing remarkable but here and there half month freeze $1000 off every once in a while.
It just, it's as, you know, it's early in the season and we're going to hit our stride in May June that I anticipate probably that stuff will slow down or diminish if we're doing our job.
Um, but right now, yeah, we have some markets that are introducing mild concessions.
Yeah, it's funny.
I, I, I'm, I'm based out of, uh, Scottsdale, Arizona and I think between here Nashville, Tennessee, a couple Florida markets there, it's, it's a bloodbath.
It's so much supply coming on.
So, I mean, it's, I, I'm seeing like two months free, I think down in, in Tennessee, the most significant I saw was, uh, four months free.
I, I'm always curious.
I don't like that.
I know 2008, 2009, we were given 23 months off and we still couldn't, uh, land all the deals.
It was, it was a tough time to be in business.
So, yeah, I feel for them,
um, I guess with, with pricing then since you're doing it internally or is it kind of like a, as like a, a needed basis?
Like, I mean, if your property is doing well, you're fine or do you look at the market and you, and you react, I guess how does, how does setting rates go?
And is it done?
Is it looked at, you know, kind of like monthly, quarterly or are you doing that as, as, as needed?

Uh Basically how we have it set up is a colleague and I uh we review all the assets each week and we'll do, we have a Tuesday hands in call with um regional or managers of each of those uh territories and we'll go over velocity pricing.
We do stair step pricing value pricing.
Um So it's not as, uh if, if we're doing it well, if there's 10 apartments available, you should see 10 different price points on that sheet.

Um So we, we stagger our pricing so that we have a little more share of voice in each price bracket and then we'll review that weekly minimum and then it can be every couple days if there's some velocity spike.
We'll go back and retool Nice makes sense.
And all of that data there, where are you getting kind of all that comp data from
uh every week?

The teams will send us in what has happened for the week and then we have in, you know, in trotter, we'll be tracking leads and leases so that we can look at by floor plan or by unit type if we want to and then we'll look at, you know what we've budgeted or our, you know what we're hoping to achieve and see where we're at.
Nice.
Very cool.
Um So I think that's kind of gonna be our main value and I think we can centralize all of your comp data.
Um I'll let you know, do you know much about apartment IQ?
It's been a minute since I, I have had the demos in the past and it's been a minute.
So I um and Sander would know nothing so you can give your elevator pitch for us to get a little more familiar.
I'm new to the whole industry.
So awesome.
Very cool.
So I'll let you, it's an easy conversation, which is great.
I always say that there's four things that make this product really unique.
Uh The first thing is that we're fully automated, so there's zero human manual input in this whatsoever.
I mean, we're not going out there and collecting comp data and recording it.
Your on site teams aren't calling around or, or visiting websites and putting into an Excel spreadsheet.
So, so fully automated.
Um The second thing and I think the best thing is that we actually update our data every single day.
So as you're aware, you know, properties out there, they're using these rep tools, they're changing rates regularly.
So our goal is for you to, for you to be able to jump into our platform and see exactly what's going on in the marketplace today.
Um The third thing that's great about us is we can actually track any property out there as long as they have a website and use a property management software.
They do not have to be our customers whatsoever.
And then the fourth thing that's great is um everything that we present to you an apartment IQ is 100% publicly avail available data.

So if you're keeping kind of track of the lawsuit right now, the real page lawsuit now the yard lawsuit centered around uh the, the sharing of private data, we have nothing to do with that whatsoever.
So everything in apartment IQ is what a renter would see whenever he or she would visit your property website or visit a competitor's website.

So if you don't mind sharing does do those lawsuits sort of hold any sort of effect the way you guys are making decisions or a trajectory going forward, sort of the utilization of private data or not.
I know this is kind of resonating with a lot of PM CS across the industry right now and there's some trends we're seeing.
But, well, yeah, you know, obviously the, the big lawsuit that's out there with um the pricing tools that kind of validates our, our efforts to not use pricing tools.
And then yes, the, the, the public data privacy data stuff we all knew that was coming with the California laws years ago.
I mean, that has been on its way across the country for years.
So if you didn't get on board with following what California was doing, shame on you quite honestly.
Yeah.
No, II I not surprised.
Sure.

Yeah, I actually have a list right now of 11 of the top 30 N MH C that are no longer participating in any like kind of call arounds or, or survey swaps just because of the, the issues with the, the lot of there.
So I think without an automated solution, it's gonna be harder and harder to get a lot of the comp data.
So that's an advantage for our, for our tool for sure.
Um Let me ask you this final question.
I'll kind of walk you through it.

Like are you using like a visualization tool like Power Bi I, I guess.
How, how, how do you pervert it to uh ingest the data.
Um Generally Excel um is what we have just because it's what the site teams are comfortable in.
We have some bis uh stuff here, Sandra that I don't know if you ever saw Sam, what he built for us?

Uh We have a uh one of our team members, he, he did some coding for us a couple of years ago so that he could pull data out of Intrata and Excel and a few other platforms that we use and, and put it into a B I tool for us.
But um but really the nuts and bolts that we're looking at on Tuesdays uh I don't need a prettiness.
I don't need graphs.
I've been doing it for so long.
We just look at the, we just look at the numbers on the Excel sheets.
Cool makes sense.
Yeah.

One being our kind of like our in app experience.
Um everything is available via CS V or Excel.
And then for like that, that in house B I tool, you have, we do have API integrations available.

Um This market survey report which we're on, this is more so like kind of understanding the market at a high level and then also understanding uh positioning, you know, like where do your one bedroom fit in comparison to the competition?
And then then this unit report which I'll get into.

So I'll, I'll show you this one a little bit more in depth.

I think very representative of your standard market survey, we simply list all of your competitors across the top and then we also include your property that way you can easily make comparisons and then all of the data is accessible via vertical scrolling.
So two different sections here.
The first one being property summary, it's all of your high level property data.

Um We show that over a couple of different time horizons.
So total exposure, exposure exposure over the next 30 next 60 days.
Um Down here, you'll see that we provide some transactional data.

So the number of leases that each property had over the last 30 days over the last seven days and the number of leases that each property averaged per week as well as the total total number of units that are available in each property and of those units that are available, how many are actually vacant as well as lastly?
And and maybe most importantly, the the uh concessions out there.
So we will let you know if there is a rent special or concession attached to a property or attached to a specific unit in the property.

And from here, we do take it a step further and we will calculate net effective rent and that effective price per foot for you at the property level at the floor plan level and all the way down to the unit level itself as well.
So I guess for this first section here at a high level, are, are we kind of missing anything?
I I know you mentioned the um like the uh the tour information that you get from intrata, that that is something we can't access.
But I think everything else here is, is stuff that we're able to hit on.
No that's great.
Just Justin, I'm curious, um, looking at this, who, who do you consider your largest competitor in this space?
It was a company called Radix.
So, they're actually based not too far from me here in Scottsdale.
Um, they were kind of the, uh, the only game in town here for probably 78 years.
Uh, the way their system works if you're not familiar is, is they have, um, they call, they're called survey swaps.

And basically what happens is every Monday, your team would have to get in there and fill out your information and then your competition would have to get in there and fill out all their, their information and then it was all shared.
So it's kind of like a giant shared Google sheet.
Um that was relying on you filling out your information timely and accurately.
Um It's like the the star reports for hotels, but just looking at this reminded me a little bit of the costar product that we used to use years ago.
It, it is a little similar to the costar product.
Yeah.

And, and honestly, whenever we built this, we kind of took aspects of everything that, you know, the industry was familiar with and tried to make it as, as seamless as possible to, you know, to, to navigate and to understand the the data flow.
Yeah, the biggest thing is, is again gonna be the automation.
So like with our, our competitor, Radis, for example, if you fill it out on a Monday, that that is out of date the very next day just because of these pricing tools and, and everything there.
Um and also too, I mean, II I have some customers that came over.

They were saying like, hey, you know, we would fill out our information on Monday morning, it would be accurate, but then our rev tool would change prices at 10 a.m. So an hour later, the data was wrong.
I mean, they were technically right, but they were technically telling the truth, but it was kind of like their competitor on that day, right?
And then same as concessions to it.
Hey, we, you know, we weren't necessarily in including all of our concessions.

And then the latest one is, is the issue with the uh the real page lawsuit is that a lot of um the radi uh participants were no longer filling out their information at all because they didn't want to be involved with the sharing of private data.
So it's been kind of a, a nice thing for us being honest.
But yeah, we automate everything.
I mean, the data that we would show in apartment IQ it's coming directly again from those property websites.
So that's what a renter is seeing if he or she visits that property website.
And I think the nicest thing about this is like every data point metric calculation I can trace back to the source.
Like if you say, hey, where's this metric coming from?
I can pull up the property website and I could show you exactly where it's coming from.
So there's no, you know,
guessing if, hey, was this a true number is an arbitrary number or anything like that?

Um Down below here, then as far as pricing availability goes, I think that this is a, a really easy way to understand exactly where, you know, each of your floor plans fit compared to the competition.
So this is broken up by floor plans.

So we're looking at one bedrooms, two bedrooms, three bedrooms, obviously, if your property, your competitors have studios, four bedrooms, five bedrooms, those would be included as well inside of here.
You know, we give you every key metric to help understand where your one bedrooms are positioned.
So we'll show you the gross rent.

Let you know if there is a concession attached to this specific floor plan, we'll show you net effective rent, square footage price per foot, net effective price per foot and the number of one bedrooms that each property has available.
So the whole idea here is to sort it.
So whether you care about, you know, analyzing the space off of rent or price per foot, you can sort it from high to low.
You can see you as the most expensive one bedrooms, you can see who is the least expensive one bedrooms.
And then you can see that our one bedrooms are the third most expensive out of the eight in our comp set.
So again, is this in line with our strategy, you know, for this, for this example, let's pretend that we're worried about occupancy.
Let's say that, hey, you know, we've got a ton of availability for our one bedrooms.
We're gonna start leasing them up.
We can look down the list here and we can see that, hey, everybody else is offering some kind of a concession for their one bedrooms.
That could be our trigger to to start offering that concession or conversely, if you say, hey, you know what?
We're, we're happy.
We only have three or 41 bedrooms left available.
Let's try to, you know, push rent rates or even try to achieve a higher rent.
We're able to see kind of what the ceiling is and what the market is willing to bear right now in terms of those one bedrooms.

So just an easy data flow as far as understanding your market and then communicating it internally, whether it be with, you know, regionals or or on site team members or, or even with clients and ownership groups.
Any questions here,
no, seems pretty straightforward.
Yeah, cool.
And then I think the probably the most powerful report here is gonna be this unit report.
So like I was saying before, it's every single unit that's currently available in your comp set right now.
So data down to this most granular level um I did mention but we calculate net effective rent and net effective price per foot at this unit level.
We also, which is pretty cool.
We also show you the number of rate changes that each unit's made over the last 30 days.
So I sort of this from high to low.
But the reason I do this is to emphasize the importance of daily data.
So you can see that this unit's made 24 rate changes over the last 30 days, 21 rate changes and 20 rate changes and so on.
So again, you're trying to understand your market.
You know, we're gonna show you exactly what's going on today rather than on Monday or last week or last month.
But the idea with this report here is to essentially utilize these filters at the top to manipulate all the data down below to answer questions based off of these key metrics.
So these cards are based off of things like days on market rent, price, price per foot square footage and total number of units.
Um The example I always use here is uh just kind of AAA quick use case is in your market.
Let's pretend there's a very wide range of one bedrooms, let's say they range from 500 all the way up to 1000 square feet.
And let's pretend that you're specifically only interested in one bedrooms that fall between 507 100 square feet.
What you can do here is you can toggle on one bedrooms, then you go to the size filter you can type in 500 to 700 ft.
You click apply and all the data is gonna populate based on units that meet that criteria.
So you can see that right now in our entire comps that there's 12 units out there that meet that criteria.
The average price per foot for those units is a dollar 92 a foot.
Average rent price is 1288 1288 a month.
And down below here, this is the unit level data for all 12 of those one bed between 507 100 square feet.
So really taking this large sum of data flying these filters and, and getting down to actually what you're, what you're looking for.
Do you think,
do you think a report like that Sarah would be sort of helpful as far as understanding market positioning and you know, down to the granularity of, you know, your unit level insights as well.
Yeah, I mean, sure any any data is helpful.

Um Yes, we the the prior document, we have something relatively similar in house, but this one's nice that um uh getting into the weeds a little bit if you really needed to get a little bit more nuance specifically, maybe with a lease up or something like that.
So you can really kind of position yourself for your absorption.
Sure.
Yeah, absolutely.

Even too as far as absorption rate or not, I guess to understanding, you know what's on the market like utilizing these, these filters, you can look at, you know, if you're only interested in exposure for two bedrooms or exposure for, for one bedrooms or if you're looking at, you know, velocity, you can look at like velocity by floor plan two just by applying these filters.
Now remind me we're, we're giving you the comp that we want you to, to study.

Are you also then giving us recommended comp I know some of the partners I work with, they'll give me back, you know, a list of comps that are regionally driven or a list of digitally driven comps that maybe are the ones that are getting clicked on the same in a certain geography.
Um Is that something that you all do as well?
It is.
Yeah, absolutely.
Yeah.
So, so the way we set these up is you can give us a list of your comps and we can build it that way.
We also have a comp matching algorithm where essentially what we do is we look at every property in the vicinity of your property and then we assign an overall match score to it.
So these scores they go from 0 to 100 100 would be the best match with the best comp ever.
A zero would be the worst.
Um A few factors that we take into account whenever we assign these scores are one location.
So proximity to your own property, we also look at year built or renovated, we look at asset class and then we uh do some amenity matching as well and then we spit out those scores.
So during the implementation process, um you could kind of sit down with your CS M and, and you could say, you know, who are you guys recommending that we, that we add to our comp set?
And we could say, well, you know, property A B and C, they're an 85 and above, we think you should add them.

So, since it's automated and, and you know, if you're in those concessions are popping up, I think it's valuable to add, you know, a couple extra comps just because it doesn't take any more time, but it's totally up to you if you want to add them.
And then, you know, two weeks later you say you don't want no, it's not a reel a relevant comp, you can always remove them anytime you'd like as well.
So you just click the X and you can take them out of your comp set.
But yeah, long story short, we do make comp recommendations.
Thank you.
Yep.
Um Yeah, I was gonna show you too that we have some reporting.
So the reporting is available uh via PDF here.
So these things are proper specific.
They are downloadable anytime you'd like as well.
Um We also time stamp them that way whoever is looking at the data is on the same page as far as whenever it's been pulled.

and you also get a unit type analysis for every floor plan that's available in your comp set.

And then lastly, we do provide some concession information.
So how many of your properties or how many properties in your comp that are offering concessions?
What's the average value of those concessions?

So it's literally as easy as giving us a list of your properties their preferred comp.
So we build it.
So turnaround time is, is, is days, not weeks,
right?

I would imagine.
So I would imagine anything that takes a little bit of that um
uh manual labor out of things, uh would be helpful then it comes down to, um, would they even pay attention to the data when it's automatically generated for them?
Because part of the part of the manual process is deliberate to force people to put eyes on content that matters.

Yeah.
And, and what's nice about that?
I'll let you know is we do offer a bunch of different training.
So we do group sessions, uh one on one sessions, ongoing training sessions to get new employees.
Our team does a really nice job of kind of speaking to the on site teams and telling them how to kind of use this rather than their traditional process, like a few use cases too.
I mean, you start hearing like, hey, concessions are popping up everywhere.
Well, it's one of those things.
Are they offering $1000 off a, off a rent or are they just waiving app fees?

So for them understanding the context of the concessions that are in the marketplace or even to like if as far as strategy goes, like if they say, hey, you know what, you know, we're at one per, we're at 99% occupancy, let's lease out this final one or two units and, and, and people starting, you know, they start talking to renters and they say, you know, we're we're overpriced.
Well, looking at this and, and helping them understand like, hey, you know what, we only have one or two units available.
Everybody else has 1020 30 units available.
So their exposure is a lot different.
So they have to lower their prices to to kind of fill up those vacancies.
Whereas with us, like we have one or two units available.
So let's try to achieve that higher rent price.
So it's kind of like taking the different pieces of the puzzle, putting it together.
And then also too, it's nice that they do want to keep in contact with, with the, with their competition and do that call around.
Um I mean, you can click on these images at the top and we'll give them some detailed information right here as well.

So if they wanna, you know, visit the property website or even visit the Google Maps or, or see every single unit that is available, um This information is, is accessible with a couple clicks of a button.
So I think um I, I guess in terms of, you know, taking the the collection process off their hands.

Sure.
Well, thank you.
I appreciate you showing us your product.
